questionHtml:
Which species contains nitrogen with the highest oxidation state?

A. NO3−

B. NO2−

C. NO2

D. N2O

meta.title: Which species contains nitrogen with the highest oxidation state? A. NO3− B. NO2− C. NO2 D. N2O
breadcrumbLabels: Structure 3.1—The periodic table: Classification of elements

Structure 3.1.6—The oxidation state is a number assigned to an atom to show the number of electrons transferred in forming a bond. It is the charge that atom would have if the compound were composed of ions. Deduce the oxidation states of an atom in an ion or a compound.
